release_findings: The archive includes final authored data and generation/evaluation code but no paper-run responses, judgments, usage, costs, invalid rows, repeated calls, or result tables.

The paper's trigger/target/scope/state-transition/evidence description is only partially projected into released operation rows: trigger, target, old_value, new_value and evidence exist, but scope and explicit before/after lifecycle state do not.

The retained 403 conversations and 2006 question pairs match the paper. The archive does not preserve the paper-reported 727 generated candidates or all rejection attempts, so the 55.4 percent retention process cannot be independently audited.

The shell evaluator defaults to GPT-4o as stated in the paper, while the Python evaluator's direct-invocation default is GPT-4.1-mini; exact paper command, endpoint, prompt hash, response corpus and judge records are absent.

The long-context injection code supports an optional seed, but no paper-run seed or frozen carrier-selection manifest separate from the released final records is reported. The released final long-context records themselves are inspectable.
No human item validation, human-judge calibration, judge-repeat study, repeated model trials, confidence intervals or cluster-aware uncertainty artifacts are released.
execution: Static inspection only; no release code, untrusted dependency, model endpoint, managed-memory service or paid API was executed.
